topic: LLM Context Window Comparison 2026: GPT-4o vs Claude 3.5 vs Gemini 1.5

competitors.title: LLM Context Window Comparison (2026): Every Model, Priced and Benchmarked
key_points: Context windows have grown from 4K tokens to 10M (Llama 4 Scout)
Gemini 2.5 Pro: 1M context (2M beta), $1.25-2.50/M input, $10-15/M output; 2x pricing surcharge above 200K tokens
GPT-4.1: 1M context, $2.00/M input, $8.00/M output; NO pricing surcharge at any length — competitive advantage
Claude Opus 4.6: 200K context (1M beta), $5.00/M input, $25.00/M output; 2x surcharge above 200K applies to ALL tokens
DeepSeek V3: 128K context, $0.14/M input, $0.28/M output — cheapest frontier model
Anthropic/Google surcharges at 200K+ apply to ENTIRE request, not just overage — 201K request costs 2x a 199K request
RULER benchmark: Gemini 1.5 Pro loses only -2.3 pts from 4K to 128K; GPT-4 loses -15.4 pts; Llama 3.1-70B loses -29.9 pts
Context rot: every model degrades with longer input — Chroma tested 18 models and found universal degradation
Models perform BETTER on shuffled text than coherent text due to reduced recency bias
Cost at 1B tokens/month: $100 (Gemini Flash-Lite) to $5,000 (Claude Opus 4.6) — 35x spread

competitors.title: Best LLM APIs in 2026: Comparing OpenAI, Claude, Gemini, Azure, Bedrock, Mistral & DeepSeek
key_points: 1M+ context windows are now standard across all major flagships in 2026
Agentic features (tool calling, computer use, multi-step workflows) are now expected, not nice-to-have
GPT-5.4/GPT-5.3 Instant: 1M context, <250ms TTFT, 77 TPS, $2-8/M tokens
Claude Opus 4.6/Sonnet 4.6: 200K context (1M beta), <300ms TTFT, 65 TPS, $3-25/M tokens
Gemini 2.5 Pro/Flash: 1M+ context, <180ms TTFT, 101 TPS, $0.15-15/M tokens
OpenAI has broadest ecosystem; Claude best for agentic workflows and code; Gemini dominates multimodal long-context
Cost spread widened dramatically: DeepSeek 5-10x cheaper than frontier alternatives
Batch API can cut costs ~50% for offline workloads; prompt caching cuts 40-60% for repeated system prompts
Production failures rarely from wrong model choice — usually from operational mismatch (rate limits, pricing, deprecation)
DeepSeek V3/R1: 128K context, <150ms TTFT, 110+ TPS — fastest and cheapest, but limited enterprise compliance

competitors.title: ChatGPT vs. Claude vs. Google Gemini Models: Full Report and Comparison (September 2025 Update)
key_points: GPT-5: Dual-mode reasoning router (fast vs deep thinking), SOTA on coding/math/science benchmarks
GPT-4.1: 1M-token context, 54.6% SWE-Bench, excels at multi-file coding and agentic tool use
GPT-4o: 128K context, first fully multimodal GPT (text+vision+audio), ~0.32s voice latency
Claude Opus 4: 72.5% SWE-Bench (best at launch), 100K+ context with 'memory files' extending effective recall
Claude 4.1 Opus: 74.5% SWE-Bench, 98.76% safe-response rate, better state-tracking for agents
Gemini 2.5 Pro: 1M+ context (2M planned), #1 on LMArena human preference, 63.8% SWE-Bench
Gemini 2.5 Flash: First 'thinking-enabled' Flash model, 1M context, $0.30/M input, $2.50/M output
Multimodality: GPT-5 native audio+vision; Gemini full-spectrum (image editing, video, voice); Claude text-first with external tools
Enterprise: Azure OpenAI/AWS Bedrock excel at compliance; direct API best for developer velocity
GPT-5 Pro tier: 22% fewer major errors, highest-scoring OpenAI model for scientific/technical tasks

competitors.title: LLM Context Window Comparison - ChatGPT vs Claude vs Gemini Context Limits (107 Models Tracked)
key_points: Tracks 107+ models with context windows, pricing, and value scores
Qwen Long Latest: 10M tokens context, $0.07/M input — largest context available
Grok 4/4.1 Fast: 2M tokens context, $0.20-3.00/M input — competitive long-context option
Gemini 2.0 Flash: 1M tokens, $0.10/M input — best value among 1M+ context models
Context window includes input + output + conversation history — not just input capacity
Reasoning models (GPT-5.2, Gemini 3 Pro) use internal tokens that reduce effective conversation capacity
100K document in 128K GPT-4o leaves only 28K for conversation (~15-20 responses before reset)

stats.fact: Context windows grew from 4K tokens to 10M tokens between 2023-2026, a 2,500x increase
Gemini 1.5 Pro loses only -2.3 points on RULER benchmark from 4K to 128K context (best retention)
GPT-4 loses -15.4 points on RULER from 4K to 128K context
Llama 3.1-70B loses -29.9 points and Mixtral-8x22B loses -63.9 points on RULER from 4K to 128K
Anthropic and Google charge 2x input surcharge when context exceeds 200K tokens — applies to ALL tokens, not just overage
OpenAI has NO pricing surcharge at any context length — competitive advantage for long-context workloads
1B tokens/month costs $100 (Gemini Flash-Lite) to $5,000 (Claude Opus 4.6) — 35x cost spread across providers
Claude Opus 4.6 achieves 72.5-74.5% SWE-Bench, highest coding accuracy among publicly benchmarked models
GPT-4.1 achieves 54.6% SWE-Bench with 1M context, far above GPT-4o's 33%
Gemini 2.5 Pro scores 63.8% SWE-Bench and tops LMArena human preference rankings
1M+ context windows are now standard across all major flagships in 2026 (GPT-4.1, Claude 4.6 beta, Gemini 2.5 Pro)
Batch API processing can cut costs ~50% for offline workloads; prompt caching saves 40-60% for repeated system prompts
DeepSeek V3/R1 offers 5-10x cheaper pricing than frontier alternatives at $0.14-0.55/M input tokens
Every LLM tested shows degradation with longer context — Chroma's study of 18 models found universal performance drop
Models perform better on shuffled text than coherent text due to reduced recency bias in attention patterns

angles: Context window ≠ effective context: Why advertised token counts mislead and how RULER benchmarks reveal real performance
The 200K pricing cliff: How Anthropic and Google's surcharges double costs for long-context workloads while OpenAI doesn't
Context rot is real: Every model degrades at longer contexts, and ordering your prompt content strategically matters more than you think
RAG vs long context in 2026: When to use RAG even with 1M token windows (hint: cost and quality both matter)
The agentic context problem: Why coding agents burn through 150K+ tokens per session and how compression saves 50%+ costs
Budget vs premium context: Gemini Flash ($0.15/M) vs Claude Opus ($5.00/M) — when 35x cost difference is worth it
Multimodal context matters: Gemini processes video/audio natively in context while Claude remains text-first — implications for developers
outline: Introduction: Why Context Windows Matter More Than You Think in 2026
The 2026 Context Window Landscape: From 4K to 10M Tokens
Head-to-Head: GPT-4o/GPT-4.1 vs Claude 4/4.6 vs Gemini 2.5 Pro/Flash
Context Window Specs Compared: Advertised vs Effective (RULER Benchmarks)
The 200K Pricing Cliff: Hidden Surcharges That Double Your Bill
Context Rot: Why More Tokens Can Mean Worse Output
Cost at Scale: $100/Month vs $5,000/Month for 1B Tokens
Multimodal Context: Text, Images, Audio, and Video Capabilities
Agentic Workflows: Context Compression and Memory Management
RAG vs Long Context: When to Use Which Strategy
Enterprise Considerations: Compliance, Rate Limits, Deprecation
Decision Framework: Choosing the Right Model for Your Context Needs
Future Outlook: 2M+ Context Windows and What They Enable
